lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Teacher, can I ask something about the sentence you wrote? I still mix up when to use 了.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Of course. Which sentence?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: 我昨天买了一本书。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ zuótiān mǎi le yì běn shū.
lines.ru_note: 'Вчера я купил книгу' — завершённое действие в прошлом

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: This one. Is 了 here just because it's in the past?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Not quite — 昨天 already tells us it's the past. 了 is there because the buying is finished, a completed event.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: So if I say 'I bought books every day last week,' would I still use 了?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Usually not, because that's a repeated, habitual action, not one finished event. 了 fits single completed actions better.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: That helps a lot. One more question — can I drop 了 if the time word is already there?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Sometimes, in casual speech, but in writing it's safer to keep it when the action is truly completed. Practice with a few more examples for homework.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Will do. Thanks for clearing that up.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Today's grammar point is the 把 construction. It's used when you do something to an object and change it in a specific way.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: So it's just a different word order for 'I did something to the object'?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Right. Instead of Subject-Verb-Object, you get Subject-把-Object-Verb, and the verb usually needs something extra after it — a result, a direction, or 了.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: 请把门关上。
lines.ru_note: 'Пожалуйста, закрой дверь' — конструкция 把 требует результата после глагола

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: 'Please close the door.' Why can't I just say 请关门 instead?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: You can say that too, but 把 emphasizes what happens to the specific door — that it ends up closed. It highlights the result on a known object.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Can I use 把 with any verb?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: No, only with verbs that can affect or change the object — moving it, breaking it, finishing it. You can't use it with verbs like 'like' or 'see.'

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: 他把作业写完了。

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: 'He finished writing the homework.' I see — 完 shows the result, and 了 shows it's done.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Exactly right. Try turning three sentences from your textbook into the 把 form for next class.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Sure, I'll give it a try tonight.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: 老师，这个字怎么念？

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Good question. That word is 睡觉, 'to sleep.' Try saying it.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: shui jiao
lines.ru_note: Ученик произносит без чёткого тона — учитель поправит ниже

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Close, but your first syllable came out flat. It should fall sharply — fourth tone, like a short command: shuì.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Like this? Shuì.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Better! Now the second syllable, 觉, is also fourth tone — jiào. Both syllables fall.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: 睡觉

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Much clearer. Remember, if the tone is wrong, it can sound like a completely different word, so it's worth the extra practice.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I understand. I'll practice this word a few more times tonight.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Great attitude. Listening and repeating slowly really helps with tones.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Sorry, teacher, could you repeat that? You went a bit fast for me.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: No problem at all. I said: open your books to page twenty and look at exercise two.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Page twenty, got it. And which exercise, sorry?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Exercise two. It's the one with the pictures of family members.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Found it. Could you also say the instructions one more time, more slowly?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Sure. Look. At. The. Picture. Then. Write. The. Correct. Word.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Perfect, that's much clearer now. Thank you for slowing down.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Anytime — just raise your hand whenever I speak too fast. It happens to every teacher.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I will. It's a useful phrase to know for other classes too.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: It is. You can also say 'Could you say that again?' or 'One more time, please.'

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Before you leave, let's go over this week's homework requirements carefully.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: OK, I'm ready to write it down.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: First, write a short paragraph about your weekend, at least eighty characters, using 了 at least twice.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Eighty characters minimum, understood. Does punctuation count toward that?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: No, only the characters themselves. Second, record yourself reading the dialogue on page fifteen and send me the audio.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Should I send it through the class group or email?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: The class group is fine, just tag me so I don't miss it.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Got it. Is there a deadline for the recording too?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Same as the paragraph — Monday morning, before class. Late submissions lose participation points.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Understood, I'll finish both over the weekend.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Great. If you're unsure about a sentence, it's fine to ask in the group chat too.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Thanks, that's reassuring. Have a good weekend, teacher.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Excuse me, I think I lost my library card. Can I get a new one?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Sure, that happens often. When did you last see it?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Maybe two days ago, in the reading room. I checked my bag many times.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: OK, I'll cancel the old card first, so no one else can use it. Can I have your student ID number?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Yes, it's on my student ID card here.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Thanks. There's a small replacement fee, ten yuan. Is that OK?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: That's fine. Can I pay with my phone?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Yes, just scan the code at the counter. I'll print your new card while you pay.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Done. How long until the new card works?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: It's active right away. Here you go — try it at the gate before you leave, just to be sure.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Will do. Thank you for the quick help.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: No problem. Try to keep it in the same pocket every time — that helps a lot of students.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Hey Li Wei, do you have a minute? I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Li Wei
lines.text: Sure, what's up?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: A few of us are starting a study group for the grammar exam. Would you like to join?

lines.speaker: Li Wei
lines.text: That sounds great, I've been struggling with 了 and 过. When do you meet?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Tuesdays and Thursdays, seven o'clock, in the library study room.

lines.speaker: Li Wei
lines.text: I have a class until seven on Tuesdays. Could I just come on Thursdays?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Of course, come whenever you can. It's not mandatory every time.

lines.speaker: Li Wei
lines.text: Perfect. Should I bring anything?

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: Just your notes and maybe some practice questions if you find good ones.

lines.speaker: Li Wei
lines.text: I'll do that. Thanks for inviting me, I really need the extra practice.

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: Good morning. How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Good morning. I just moved into the dorm and I was told I need to register my residence within twenty-four hours.

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: That's right. Since you live in the dormitory, the university handles that for you automatically, but I still need your passport and visa page to confirm.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Here's my passport. I'm on an X1 student visa.

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: Thank you. I also see your visa expires in about five months — you'll need a residence permit before then, not just the visa.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Sorry, what's the difference between the visa and the residence permit?
lines.ru_note: residence permit — вид на жительство (для учёбы, оформляется после приезда)

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: The visa got you into the country. The residence permit lets you stay legally for the length of your program — usually a full academic year.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I see. What documents do I need for the residence permit application?

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: Your passport, the admission letter, the physical exam report, and this registration form. We'll also need two passport photos.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Where do I get the physical exam done?

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: At the international travel health center downtown. I'll give you the address and a letter of introduction.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Thank you. How long does the whole process usually take?

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: About two to three weeks once everything is submitted, so please don't leave it until the last moment.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Understood, I'll go to the health center this week and come back with everything.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Hi, I'd like to buy a SIM card for my phone, please.

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Sure. Are you a student? We have a special data plan for students.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Yes, I'm a student. What does the plan include?

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Thirty gigabytes of data and two hundred minutes of calls, for fifty-eight yuan a month.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: That sounds good. What do I need to register it?

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Just your passport, and I'll take a photo for the real-name registration system.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Here you go. Can I keep my old phone number from my home country too?

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: This will be a new Chinese number, but your old SIM still works if you switch it back later.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: 多少钱？

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Fifty-eight yuan for the first month, plus a twenty-yuan card fee, one time only.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: OK, I'll take it. Can I pay by phone transfer?

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Yes, scan this code. Your SIM should be active in about ten minutes.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Great, thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Come in, please. What seems to be the problem today?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I've had a sore throat and a headache since yesterday, and I feel a bit tired.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Do you have a fever? Let me check your temperature.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I think so — I felt hot last night, but I don't have a thermometer.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Thirty-seven point eight. A mild fever. Any coughing or stomach trouble?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: A little cough, no stomach problems. Is it serious?

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: It looks like a common cold. Nothing serious, but let's do a quick throat check to be safe.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: 张嘴，啊——
lines.ru_note: 'Открой рот, а——' — стандартная фраза врача при осмотре горла

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Like this?

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Good, a bit red but nothing worrying. I'll prescribe some medicine for the fever and throat.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: How should I take it?

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: One tablet after breakfast and one after dinner, for three days. Drink plenty of warm water and rest.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Should I come back if it doesn't get better?

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: Yes, come back if the fever is still there after three days, or gets worse before that.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Hi, I placed an order online a few minutes ago — pickup, not delivery.

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Sure, what's the order number or the name on the order?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: It should be under my phone number, ending in 6688.

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: One moment. Ah yes — noodles with beef and an iced tea, correct?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Yes, that's mine. Is it ready?

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Almost, it needs about three more minutes. Would you like extra chili on the side?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: No thanks, it's already spicy enough for me.

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Got it. Do you need chopsticks and a spoon, or do you have your own?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Chopsticks and a spoon, please.

lines.speaker: Seller
lines.text: Here you are — noodles, iced tea, chopsticks, spoon. Enjoy your meal!

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: 谢谢，麻烦了。
lines.ru_note: 'Спасибо, извините за беспокойство' — вежливая фраза при получении заказа

title: Taking a DiDi to the Station

lines.speaker: Driver
lines.text: Hi, are you the one who ordered a car to the train station?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Yes, that's me. Thanks for coming.

lines.speaker: Driver
lines.text: No problem, hop in. Which station — the east one or the south one? The app just says 'train station.'

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: The south station, sorry, I should have typed the full name.

lines.speaker: Driver
lines.text: No worries, I'll update the route. Traffic looks fine, should take about twenty-five minutes.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Great, my train leaves in an hour, so that should be enough time.

lines.speaker: Driver
lines.text: Plenty of time. First time taking the train from here?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Yes, actually. Do I need to arrive very early?

lines.speaker: Driver
lines.text: About thirty minutes before departure is fine, but security lines can be long on weekends.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Good to know, thanks. Can you drop me at the north entrance? I saw online that it's less crowded.

lines.speaker: Driver
lines.text: Sure thing. We're almost there — that'll be twenty-six yuan total.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: It should be paid through the app already, right?

lines.speaker: Driver
lines.text: Yes, it's already settled. Here we are — safe travels!

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: 谢谢师傅！
lines.ru_note: 'Спасибо, мастер!' — вежливое обращение к водителю такси

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Let's review a few mistakes from your last exam before we move on to new material.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: OK, I think I got the 把 sentence wrong. Can we look at that one?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Sure. You wrote 我把书看了 for 'I read the book.' Can anyone spot the issue?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I'm not sure — I thought 了 was enough to show it's finished.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: The verb needs a stronger result complement with 把, not just 了 alone. 看完 — 'finished reading' — works much better here.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: 我把书看完了。
lines.ru_note: 'Я дочитал книгу' — правильный вариант с результативным дополнением

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: I see, so plain 了 without a result complement doesn't sound complete in a 把 sentence.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Exactly. Now let's look at question two — the measure word mistake most of you made.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: That was the one about two tickets, right? I wrote 两个票 instead of 两张票.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Right, tickets are flat, so they take 张, not the general 个. Small mistake, but it stands out to a native speaker.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Got it. Is there a list of common measure-word mistakes we could review before the next test?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Good idea, I'll put together a short handout and share it with the class this week.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: That would help a lot, thank you.
